title: Time Unit Converter (ms, s, min, h, days, weeks, months, years)
shortDescription: Convert between time units from milliseconds to years. Months use the average length, years use the Julian definition.
description: Type a duration and select its unit to see equivalents in every other supported unit. Useful for scheduling, billing, scientific calculations, and quick mental arithmetic.
metaDescription: Free time unit converter for milliseconds, seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, and years. Instant browser-based conversion using standard definitions.

sections.heading: Why months and years aren't exact
paragraphs: Seconds, minutes, hours, days, and weeks are exact: 1 minute is always 60 seconds, 1 day is always 86,400 seconds. Months and years are not — calendar months range from 28 to 31 days, and years can be 365 or 366 days. To convert at all, we have to pick a single average value.

This calculator uses the Julian year (365.25 days = 31,557,600 seconds), the standard in astronomy and the SI scientific community. The 'month' is one twelfth of a Julian year (30.4375 days = 2,629,800 seconds). These choices give consistent two-way conversions; they may differ from your calendar by a day or two.

sections.heading: What's exact and what isn't
paragraphs: Exact: ms ↔ s, s ↔ min, min ↔ h, h ↔ day, day ↔ week. These can be converted forward and back without losing any precision.

Approximate: any conversion involving 'month' or 'year', because the calendar values are not constant. If you need accurate calendar arithmetic ('how many days from March 1 to August 15'), use a date difference calculator instead — the time-unit converter is for averages.

sections.heading: Useful reference points
paragraphs: 1 year ≈ 525,960 minutes ≈ 31.56 million seconds. 1 day = 1,440 minutes = 86,400 seconds. 1 week = 168 hours. 'A million seconds' ≈ 11.6 days; 'a billion seconds' ≈ 31.7 years. 1 hour of HD video ≈ 3.6 GB; 1 hour of audio ≈ 50 MB.

howTo.name: Pick the unit
howTo.text: Select what your value is in.

howTo.name: See all units
howTo.text: Conversions to every other unit appear instantly.

faq.a: We use the Julian year, the SI standard for astronomy. It's the average over the 4-year leap cycle. Calendar years vary by ±1 day; this is the long-run average.
1/12 of a Julian year, or 30.4375 days. It does not match any specific calendar month.

faq.q: Why did 1 minute → 60.0 s but 1 month → 30.4375 d?
faq.a: Minutes are defined exactly. Months are calendar units that don't have a single fixed length, so we pick the long-run average for conversion.

faq.q: Is this accurate for billing or scheduling?
faq.a: For exact calendar arithmetic, use a date difference calculator. For averages and rates ('hours per year'), this is the right tool.
Not modeled. Leap seconds happen rarely (~1 per 1.5 years on average) and are smaller than the rounding error from any month-or-year conversion.

faq.q: How precise are the smaller units?
faq.a: ms ↔ s ↔ min ↔ h ↔ d are exact, displayed to up to 8 significant digits.

faq.q: Can I use this for video duration?
faq.a: Yes. Film and video duration is just seconds-to-minutes-to-hours, which is exact.
It converts a known duration; it doesn't measure live time. For measuring elapsed time, use a stopwatch app or our countdown timer.
